paper_title: Lake Ladoga and Lake Onega Quaternary Sediments Study Methods

methods.description: Conducting geophysical surveys using various acoustic methods (Ultra-High-Resolution Seismic Prospecting - SSVR, Ultra-High Resolution Seismic Exploration - UHRS, side-scan sonar - GLBO) to image the subsurface sediment layers, obtain a seismostratigraphic section, and identify suitable locations for sediment sampling.

steps.description: Conduct ultra-high-resolution seismic prospecting (SSVR) with a boomer source and a 16-channel analogue seismic streamer.
steps.searchable_summary: Conduct SSVR seismic survey using boomer and streamer

steps.description: Conduct ultra-high resolution seismic exploration (UHRS) with a parametric profiler SES-2000 Light.
steps.searchable_summary: Conduct UHRS seismic survey using parametric profiler

steps.description: Conduct side-scan sonar (GLBO) survey with a Klein450.
steps.searchable_summary: Conduct side-scan sonar survey

steps.description: Process and interpret seismoacoustic profiling data.
steps.searchable_summary: Process and interpret seismic and sonar data

steps.description: Obtain a general seismostratigraphic section of loose deposits based on interpretation.
steps.searchable_summary: Generate seismostratigraphic section model

steps.description: Identify places where sediments of different ages approached the bottom surface based on interpretation.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify sediment age boundaries near surface from seismic data
outputs: Map or list of locations with sediment age boundaries near surface

steps.description: Select sampling points based on the interpreted seismoacoustic profiling data.
steps.searchable_summary: Select sediment coring locations based on geophysical interpretation
inputs: Map or list of locations with sediment age boundaries near surface
outputs: List or map of selected sampling points for coring

methods.description: Collecting sediment cores from the lake bottom using different coring systems depending on the location (open water vs. ice cover) and desired core length, at locations selected based on geophysical surveys.

steps.description: Select sampling points based on the interpretation of seismoacoustic profiling data.
steps.searchable_summary: Use pre-selected sampling points from geophysical survey
inputs: List or map of selected sampling points from Seismic-Acoustic Profiling method

steps.description: For sampling from a research vessel in open water: Deploy gravity corers (3m long, 127mm diameter, 300kg weight) from R/V "Professor Zenkovich".
steps.searchable_summary: Collect sediment cores using gravity corer from research vessel

steps.description: For sampling from ice cover: Deploy a UWITEC piston coring system capable of taking cores up to 20-25m long in water depths up to 140m.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect long sediment cores using piston corer from ice cover
inputs: UWITEC piston coring system (up to 25m length, up to 140m water depth)

steps.description: Recover sediment cores.
steps.searchable_summary: Retrieve collected sediment cores

methods.description: Performing laboratory analyses on collected sediment cores to characterize their physical properties, composition, and biological content (palynology) to understand sedimentation conditions and paleogeography.

steps.description: Perform tomographic studies on selected sediment corers.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform tomography on sediment cores
outputs: Tomographic images or data of core internal structure

steps.description: Perform palynological studies on sediment samples taken from the cores.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze pollen and spores in sediment samples
outputs: Palynological data (pollen/spore counts, taxa identification, concentrations)

steps.description: Characterize lithological properties of the sediment cores (e.g., describe lithofacies, identify deposit types like glacial, fluvioglacial).
steps.searchable_summary: Describe and classify sediment core lithology

methods.description: Analyzing and integrating data from seismic surveys, sediment cores (lithology, laboratory analyses), and relevant literature to construct seismostratigraphic and lithostratigraphic models, characterize the Quaternary sediment sequence, and interpret the paleogeographic development and glacial history of the study areas.

steps.description: Analyze interpreted seismoacoustic profiling data (seismostratigraphy).
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze interpreted seismic stratigraphy
inputs: Interpreted seismostratigraphic section (from Seismic-Acoustic Profiling method)

steps.description: Analyze sediment core data (lithological descriptions, tomographic data, and palynological data).
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze sediment core lithology, tomography, and palynology data
inputs: Lithological descriptions (from Sediment Core Laboratory Analysis method)
Tomographic data (from Sediment Core Laboratory Analysis method)
Palynological data (from Sediment Core Laboratory Analysis method)

steps.description: Integrate seismostratigraphic and lithostratigraphic data.
steps.searchable_summary: Correlate seismic units with core lithology

steps.description: Integrate palynological data with lithostratigraphy and seismostratigraphy to constrain timing and environmental changes.
steps.searchable_summary: Integrate palynology data for age and paleoenvironment interpretation
inputs: Palynological data (from Sediment Core Laboratory Analysis method)

steps.description: Analyze relevant literature data, including results of previous work.
steps.searchable_summary: Review and analyze relevant scientific literature

steps.description: Characterize the entire Quaternary sediment sequence based on integrated data.
steps.searchable_summary: Describe the full Quaternary stratigraphy
inputs: Age constraints and paleoenvironmental reconstruction (from step 4)
outputs: Detailed description of Quaternary stratigraphy and units

steps.description: Interpret sedimentation conditions in post-glacial time.
steps.searchable_summary: Interpret post-glacial sedimentation processes and environments

steps.description: Interpret the paleogeographic development of Lake Onega and the White Sea in the Late Pleistocene and Holocene.
steps.searchable_summary: Reconstruct Late Pleistocene and Holocene paleogeography
